findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.requests.best-practice.use-raise-for-status
findings.description: There's an HTTP request made with requests, but the raise_for_status() utility method isn't used. This can result in request errors going unnoticed and your code behaving in unexpected ways, such as if your authorization API returns a 500 error while you're only checking for a 401.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.requests.best-practice.use-timeout
findings.description: Detected a 'requests' call without a timeout set. By default, 'requests' calls wait until the connection is closed. This means a 'requests' call without a timeout will hang the program if a response is never received. Consider setting a timeout for all 'requests'.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
findings.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.
cwe_ids: CWE-668: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.avoid_using_app_run_directly
findings.description: top-level app.run(...) is ignored by flask. Consider putting app.run(...) behind a guard, like inside a function
cwe_ids: CWE-668: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.jwt-python-exposed-credentials
findings.description: Password is exposed through JWT token payload. This is not encrypted and the password could be compromised. Do not store passwords in JWT tokens.
cwe_ids: CWE-522: Insufficiently Protected Credentials

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)
cwe_ids: CWE-522: Insufficiently Protected Credentials

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.jwt.security.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)
cwe_ids: CWE-522: Insufficiently Protected Credentials

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.
cwe_ids: CWE-489: Active Debug Code

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.pass-body-fn
findings.description: `pass` is the body of function extract_tar. Consider removing this or raise NotImplementedError() if this is a TODO

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true
findings.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'Popen' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.audit.dangerous-subprocess-use-audit
findings.description: Detected subprocess function 'Popen' without a static string. If this data can be controlled by a malicious actor, it may be an instance of command injection. Audit the use of this call to ensure it is not controllable by an external resource. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: proc = Popen([libreoffice, '--headless']) # Open empty document to wait until LibreOffice is ready

tool_specific.lines: proc = Popen([libreoffice, '--headless']) # Open empty document to wait until LibreOffice is ready

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.return-not-in-function
findings.description: `return` only makes sense inside a function
findings.code_excerpt: except OSError as error: print('ERROR', 'Could not delete temporary file'); return

tool_specific.lines: except OSError as error: print('ERROR', 'Could not delete temporary file'); return

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithm-sha1
findings.description: Detected SHA1 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. SHA1 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.return-not-in-function
findings.description: `return` only makes sense inside a function

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.return-not-in-function
findings.description: `return` only makes sense inside a function

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.correctness.no-strings-as-booleans
findings.description: Using strings as booleans in Python has unexpected results. `"one" and "two"` will return "two". `"one" or "two"` will return "one". In Python, strings are truthy, and strings with a non-zero length evaluate to True.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.useless-if-body
findings.description: Useless if statement; both blocks have the same body

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.return-not-in-function
findings.description: `return` only makes sense inside a function

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.return-not-in-function
findings.description: `return` only makes sense inside a function

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.maintainability.is-function-without-parentheses
findings.description: Is "is_active" a function or an attribute? If it is a function, you may have meant user.is_active() because user.is_active is always true.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.
cwe_ids: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.no-stringify-keys
findings.description: JSON stringify does not produce a stable key ordering, and should not be relied on for producing object keys. Consider using json-stable-stringify instead.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.correctness.no-stringify-keys
findings.description: JSON stringify does not produce a stable key ordering, and should not be relied on for producing object keys. Consider using json-stable-stringify instead.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string
findings.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.
cwe_ids: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.path-traversal.path-join-resolve-traversal
findings.description: Detected possible user input going into a `path.join` or `path.resolve` function. This could possibly lead to a path traversal vulnerability,  where the attacker can access arbitrary files stored in the file system. Instead, be sure to sanitize or validate user input first.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.lang.security.audit.detect-non-literal-fs-filename
findings.description: Detected that function argument `req` has entered the fs module. An attacker could potentially control the location of this file, to include going backwards in the directory with '../'. To address this, ensure that user-controlled variables in file paths are validated.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.browser.security.insecure-document-method
findings.description: User controlled data in methods like `innerHTML`, `outerHTML` or `document.write` is an anti-pattern that can lead to XSS vulnerabilities
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string
findings.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.injection.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`flask.render_template`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled
findings.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.
cwe_ids: CWE-489: Active Debug Code

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.problem-based-packs.insecure-transport.js-node.using-http-server
findings.description: Checks for any usage of http servers instead of https servers. Encourages the usage of https protocol instead of http, which does not have TLS and is therefore unencrypted. Using http can lead to man-in-the-middle attacks in which the attacker is able to read sensitive information.
cwe_ids: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.open-never-closed
findings.description: file object opened without corresponding close
